patches.find: The FSF absolute magnitude is imported from the 2017 BOU observation and reused as the 2026 baseline input
patches.replace: The dome's own evolution log admits the pre-FSF predecessor got 2024 eclipse latitudes uniformly wrong (v31 entry, raw-text/06-evolution.txt).
patches.rationale: Finding field closing sentence carried a forward-year (2026) architecture tangent rather than WIN-025's strongest point: the dome's own evolution log admission that the pre-FSF predecessor failed on 2024 latitudes. The evolution-log admission is in detail_verdict_text but absent from the summary-row most readers see.

patches.find: — we retract our earlier characterization of FSF as empirical
patches.rationale: The self-retraction clause is ungrounded in WIN-025's own evidence — WIN-025 never characterized FSF as empirical; that characterization was in a different WIN (resolved via EXP-230). The orphaned 'we retract' clause reads as a non-sequitur and invites the question 'what earlier characterization?' which WIN-025 cannot answer. Removing it leaves the concession intact (FSF is genuine geometry) without the misleading implication of a prior error in this WIN.

patches.find: proton precipitation deepens to mesospheric altitudes while CME-driven electron precipitation couples more strongly to the D-region
patches.replace: SPE proton precipitation (10–100 MeV) penetrates deeper into the mesosphere/D-region than CME-driven auroral electron precipitation (1–10 keV, primarily depositing energy in the E-region/thermosphere, with secondary D-region coupling via ring-current and disturbance electric-field pathways)
patches.rationale: The original sentence had the mechanism direction backwards or ambiguously worded: canonical aeronomy has SPE protons penetrating deeper (to stratospheric altitudes) than CME-driven auroral electrons (which primarily affect the E-region). The sentence asserted CME electrons couple MORE strongly to the D-region, which is not supported by standard atmospheric electrodynamics. The rewrite correctly places SPE protons as the deeper-penetrating agent and describes the CME pathway accurately, while preserving the 'SPE is supporting rather than primary evidence for G3 storm behaviour' conclusion.

patches.find: lowest possible frequency from this H(r), ~8.8 Hz
patches.replace: lowest plane-parallel axial-mode frequency from this H(r), ~8.8 Hz — a first-order lower bound
patches.rationale: The original 'lowest possible frequency' claimed the pole-only H_max gives a strict lower bound on cavity resonant frequency for any mode of the non-uniform dome geometry. This is a physical heuristic (larger uniform-H cavities support lower modes; the non-uniform cavity has H ≤ H_max everywhere so the H_max axial mode is a first-order lower bound) but not a demonstrated proof. The rewrite makes the heuristic nature explicit without weakening the argument.

patches.find: stations outside are predicted to be suppressed — a crude but falsifiable
patches.replace: stations outside are predicted to be suppressed to ≤30% of the ensemble-median in-band signal (consistent with Kim & Chang (2018)'s published attenuation envelope for latitudes outside the primary response zone) — a crude but falsifiable
patches.rationale: The out-of-band suppression prediction was unquantified. 'Predicted to be suppressed' without a number is not falsifiable — a dome defender can always claim any suppression qualifies. Adding '≤30% of ensemble-median in-band signal, consistent with Kim & Chang (2018)' provides a specific, checkable threshold grounded in the same citation already used for the in-band reference.

patches.find: a crude but falsifiable nine-number competitor to the dome's nine FSF numbers.
patches.replace: a crude but falsifiable nine-number competitor to the dome's nine FSF numbers. Across the 2026-08-12 station set, most stations sit inside the 30°–50° CGM band, so the binary classifier predicts an approximately constant amplitude for the European core with only LER (≳58° CGM) clearly outside — yielding 2–3 unique predicted values across nine stations, which is why the smooth-f(mag_lat) upgrade path is the preferred form if binned ensemble amplitudes become accessible.
patches.rationale: The binary classifier collapses to 2-3 unique predicted values across the nine 2026-08-12 European stations because most cluster inside the 30°–50° band. Without this disclosure, a dome defender can argue the 'nine-number competitor' prediction is rigged for variance (most stations get the same value). The added sentence preemptively neutralizes this argument and points to the smooth-f(mag_lat) upgrade that would address it.
